calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Erica Rosenblatt for petitioners/co-administrators Diane Brenner and Shirlee Brotherton.

Ms Rosenblatt addressed several of the concerns presented in the Examiner's notes. She stated that one of the co-administrators resided 100 miles from San Francisco, and using the probate examiner's figures the co-administrator was seeking reimbursement of travel expenses in the sum of $324. Regarding the request for extraordinary fees, shestated that it was a very complicated estate with 50 intestate heirs. As to the accountants' costs, she acknowledged that the first accountant had been unable to correctly perform the work of preparing the accounting, so a second accountant had needed to be engaged. She said that her clients were seeking a refund of fees from the first accountant. The Court indicated that there was not a sufficient showing of services to justify the requested extraordinary fees. He added that he would grant the petition, subject to reducing fees as appropriate.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-07-2016 Continued To Dec-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
